title: Class and Style Binding
content:
### Object Syntax for Classes

Dynamically toggle CSS classes with an object:

```vue
<div :class="{ active: isActive, 'text-danger': hasError }">
```

Keys are class names, values are booleans. Multiple classes can be toggled independently.

### Array Syntax for Classes

Apply an array of classes:

```vue
<div :class="[activeClass, errorClass]">
<div :class="[isActive ? activeClass : '', errorClass]">
<div :class="[{ active: isActive }, errorClass]">
```

### Inline Styles with Objects

CSS properties can be camelCase or kebab-case (in quotes):

```vue
<div :style="{ color: activeColor, fontSize: fontSize + 'px' }">
<div :style="{ 'font-size': fontSize + 'px' }">
```

### Array Syntax for Styles

Apply multiple style objects:

```vue
<div :style="[baseStyles, accentStyles]">
```

### Auto-Prefixing

Vue automatically adds vendor prefixes when a style property requires them.

### Multiple Values

Provide an array of values for a property. The last supported value is applied:

```vue
<div :style="{ display: ['-webkit-box', '-ms-flexbox', 'flex'] }">
```

quiz.question: Which syntax toggles classes based on a boolean condition?
How do you apply multiple style objects?
